database_info.name: DSPPA Amplifier Selection Theory — Standards Based
database_info.purpose: Engineering design tool for PA amplifier selection, sizing, and system integration

1_amplifier_type_selection.principle: Amplifier type must match system complexity and zone requirements

types.best_for: Single room, small shop, classroom, background music

types.features: Priority paging, EMC input, chime, ducking

types.best_for: Multi-zone: hotels, shopping centers, offices, restaurants
Professional PA, conference rooms, houses of worship

types.features: Independent zone amplification, high total power

types.best_for: Rack systems requiring efficient power, portable PA
Heavy-duty PA, outdoor events, large venues

types.features: Networked zone control, high channel density

types.best_for: Hotel rooms, residential, boutique shops, multi-room audio
types.features: Flush mount, touch screen, smart home integration

types.best_for: Live sound, studios, conference, event production
types.features: EQ per channel, effects, USB recording, balanced XLR

2_power_sizing.principle: Amplifier power must deliver required SPL at the furthest listener position

examples.recommended: MP200PIII for paging, MP250UB for BGM

2_power_sizing.rule: ALWAYS select amplifier rated power ≥ 1.5× total speaker tap wattage for clean headroom. For voice evacuation (EN54): ≥ 1.5× is mandatory per BS 5839-8.

3_70v_100v_vs_low_impedance.principle: 100V/70V constant voltage is used for distributed PA. Low impedance for hi-fi/pro audio.

comparison.how_it_works: Amplifier steps up to 100V (or 70V). Speakers have transformer tap selecting power draw.
comparison.cable: Standard 2-core cable, any gauge for short runs
comparison.quality: Good for speech and BGM. Limited bass.
comparison.best_for: Distributed PA: ceiling speakers, horn speakers, column speakers across large areas

comparison.how_it_works: Amplifier drives speakers directly at 4-16Ω. Speakers wired in series/parallel.
comparison.cable: Thick cable required for long runs (voltage drop)
comparison.best_for: Hi-fi rooms, monitoring, small areas, active speakers
comparison.dsppa_amps: Mini40, Mini60, MINI260, DM835-DM868 in-wall series

3_70v_100v_vs_low_impedance.rule: For distributed PA (>4 speakers, different rooms/zones): ALWAYS use 100V/70V. For single room hi-fi/monitoring: use low impedance (4-16Ω).

4_zone_system_design.principle: Zones allow independent volume and source control for different areas

approaches.max_speakers: All speakers get same source and volume
approaches.best_for: Single room, small shop, mosque, classroom

approaches.control: Each zone can be turned ON/OFF independently, but volume is global
approaches.best_for: Small hotel, restaurant, office with zones

approaches.control: Each zone has independent volume + source selection
approaches.best_for: Large hotel, campus, hospital, shopping mall

approaches.control: Any source can be routed to any zone with independent volume
approaches.best_for: Complex installations: airports, stadiums, mixed-use buildings

approaches.control: Each network terminal is an independent zone with network audio

5_input_priority_and_ducking.principle: Emergency and paging inputs must override background music

priority_chain.description: Emergency signal overrides ALL other inputs. Triggered by dry contact or voltage signal from fire panel.
priority_chain.dsppa_feature: EMC input on rear panel (all PIII and professional models)

priority_chain.description: Announcement overrides BGM with automatic ducking (reduces BGM volume).
priority_chain.dsppa_feature: Mic 1 priority on all mixer amplifiers

priority_chain.description: Lowest priority. Plays when no other input is active.

5_input_priority_and_ducking.ducking: When higher priority input activates, lower priority sources are attenuated (typically -20dB to -40dB) rather than muted, then restored when priority input ends.

6_class_d_vs_analog.principle: Class D amplifiers are more efficient and lighter; analog amplifiers are more robust

class_d.models: DMA series, DA series, DFX series

7_speaker_cable_sizing.principle: Cable must deliver full amplifier power to furthest speaker with <1dB loss
7_speaker_cable_sizing.fire_rated_cable: For EN54/voice evacuation systems: use fire-rated cable (IEC 60331, 2hr fire resistance)

8_rack_planning.principle: 19-inch rack space must accommodate all equipment with ventilation
8_rack_planning.ventilation: Leave 1U blank between high-power amplifiers. Ensure rear ventilation. Use fan tray for enclosed racks.
8_rack_planning.power: Calculate total AC power consumption. Size UPS accordingly. 1000W amplifier draws ~1500W from mains at full load.

9_system_integration_with_other_dsppa.principle: DSPPA amplifiers integrate with the complete DSPPA ecosystem

integration_paths.design: Match amplifier power to total speaker tap wattage. Select speaker type per room.
Network terminals include built-in amplifiers. External amplifiers for high-power zones.
EN54 certified amplifiers + speakers for voice evacuation compliance.

integration_paths.connection: Audio output from conference system to amplifier input
integration_paths.design: Conference audio fed to ceiling speakers via mixer amplifier.

integration_paths.connection: MAG6588/MAG6183C routes sources to zone amplifiers
integration_paths.design: Matrix handles source selection; amplifiers handle power per zone.
Paging mic, gooseneck, wireless — connected to mixer amplifier mic inputs.

10_compliance_standards.principle: PA amplifiers must meet safety, EMC, and performance standards

standards.scope: Voice alarm control and indicating equipment (PAVA)
Power supply for fire detection/alarm (backup battery)
Voice alarm systems — design, installation, commissioning
Sound systems for emergency purposes (replaced by EN 54-16)
European conformity and hazardous substance restriction
